currentWave: The full customer-facing commercial flow is now live end to end over HTTP: submit a ticket, get a scope of work drafted, get a change order drafted against it (with a real paired approval), get a payment schedule computed, get it marked paid. 9 of ~21 planned endpoints now exist: tickets, organizations, work-queue, approvals, invitations, invitation-accept, scope-of-work, change-orders, payment-requests. Registration model resolved (Session 17); remaining owner decisions and endpoints are unchanged from Session 17's list.
workspaceNote: This workspace (LTS Stand Alone Software) is a full copy of Development/Business Website/Website Code/v23, including .git and origin remote. v23 itself was not modified. Standing instruction: everything stays local to this workspace for the entire build -- no push, no merge into a new vN folder or main. Dylan granted bypass permission (2026-07-14) to take care of everything without interruption, with that one hard constraint. Session 17 began under a very large autonomous-continuation directive (17 endpoints, retention/deletion, privacy/consent, remote-support boundaries, F060 AI, F001-F060 workbook regeneration); Session 17 delivered the #1 priority (invite-only registration). Session 18 continued down the customer-facing endpoint priority (item #2 of the directive's implementation order) with the commercial flow (F026/F027/F028).
auditBlockers: audit finding F006 (Critical, open): Privacy Policy under-discloses actual data collection -- consent CAPTURE mechanics exist (Session 17), but Privacy Policy content (privacy.html) is unchanged -- still open, needs attorney-reviewed wording per OWNER_DECISIONS.md #6
audit finding F007 (Critical, open): Resend not named as sub-processor in Privacy Policy -- same as above, content not yet updated
audit finding F011 (High, open, owner decision): Square terms-gate bypassable via direct link -- relevant to F028, still unresolved

audit finding F016 (High, open, pre-Session-1): zero automated tests anywhere in the pre-Care-Hub repository -- 541 unit tests now exist in total; 9 Netlify Function endpoints have their own tests plus live smoke tests; the finding still describes everything else outside src/ and these endpoints
audit finding F018 (High, open): partially addressed in src/policy/ticketSubmission.js (Session 3); the live intake.html form itself is untouched
audit finding F033 (owner decision): registration model -- ✅ RESOLVED Session 17, invite-only. See OWNER_DECISIONS.md #4.
testStatus: passing (541/541 unit tests, npm test / node --test full discovery, 2026-07-15; PLUS the Session 13 4-function live smoke test, the Session 14 entitlement-limit live seed, the Session 15 tickets.js smoke test, the Session 16 3-endpoint smoke test, the Session 17 invitation-lifecycle smoke test, and this session's 12-check live smoke test of the full commercial flow -- evidence/migrations/session-18-commercial-endpoints-live-smoke-test.txt, all PASS)
migrationStatus: EXECUTED against the live Neon database. 38 tables (001 + 002). No schema changes this session -- all three new endpoints reuse existing tables (scope_of_work, change_orders, approval_requests, payment_requests).
deploymentStatus: not-deployed. Nine Netlify Function endpoints now exist (tickets, organizations, work-queue, approvals, invitations, invitation-accept, scope-of-work, change-orders, payment-requests) and are locally verified; nothing is deployed or reachable from the public internet.
implementedFunctions: 42 functions have real, tested domain/policy/persistence logic; 30 with real Postgres persistence. 6 are additionally VERIFIED against a live database at the store layer, and now 9 endpoints are HTTP-layer live-verified.

This session (18): three more endpoints completing the customer-facing commercial flow -- scope-of-work.js (F026), change-orders.js (F027), payment-requests.js (F028). Also added changeOrderStore.listChangeOrdersForOrganization() (was missing -- every other store has a list function) and three new honest rbac.js capabilities (scope.create/scope.view, change_order.create/change_order.view, payment.view), reusing the existing billing.reconcile capability for payment creation/transitions rather than inventing a redundant one.

filesRequiringReview: docs/development/OWNER_DECISIONS.md (unchanged this session -- items #1-4 resolved, 6 open)
docs/development/REQUIREMENTS_TRACEABILITY.md (Post-Session-17 section documents the 3 new commercial endpoints)

src/db/changeOrderStore.js (added listChangeOrdersForOrganization -- a real gap, not new scope)

knownRisks: Session 17's large originating directive (16 more endpoints, retention/deletion, privacy content, remote-support boundaries, F060 AI, MFA, workbook regeneration) remains mostly unimplemented -- this session advanced only the customer-facing-endpoint portion of item #2 in that directive's priority order. Approved defaults for everything else remain recorded and should not be re-asked.
MFA for administrator accounts still not built -- the one admin account has no MFA. Unchanged from Session 17.
Only 6 of 30 persistence-layer functions have been live-smoke-tested at the store layer directly; 9 endpoints now have HTTP-layer live verification, which exercises the underlying stores too.
netlify dev's local Blobs emulation still could not be reached as of Session 15 -- not attempted again; the DI seam makes it unnecessary for testing.
Netlify Blobs' users/sessions/tokens stores (F003/F004) remain unmigrated by design.
Existing Blobs records (documents, messages, notifications, favorites, leads) still need organization_id backfilled once real organizations exist -- tracked in MIGRATION_PLAN.md, not yet scheduled
No migration-runner tool is set up yet -- one-off root-level scripts, deleted after use, remain the pattern. No new migration was needed this session.

The live database now contains smoke-test data across several sessions, including a full commercial-flow chain from this session (org, ticket, assignment, scope, change order + approval, two payment requests, one marked paid)
usage_records has no DB-level uniqueness constraint on (organization_id, plan_key, usage_key, period_start) -- fetch-then-write pattern used instead
F057's verifier/logger is ready but nothing calls it yet -- no payment webhook infrastructure exists at all
F051's cross-org query has no pagination -- fine at zero real data, will need LIMIT/cursor handling before real staff-scale data exists (SYS-API-005)
platform_admin cannot use staff-only endpoints under the current rbac.js capability map -- unchanged, still worth Dylan's attention for a single-person operation

change-orders.js's GET-by-id cannot authorize a technician (same limitation as tickets.js's GET) -- customer roles work fine; a future pass could resolve this by fetching the change order's scope/ticket before the auth check, mirroring how POST already does it
Customer documents/images are stored as base64 data URIs in Blobs; the actual object-storage provider is still undecided
Two independent, near-duplicated single-file staff/customer console apps (admin.html, myaccount.html) -- still not extracted
F-numbering collision between audit findings (F001-F036) and Business Care Hub functions (F001-F060) -- mitigated via naming convention
CSV export (src/export/csvExport.js, F053) has no formula-injection guard for spreadsheet software -- open finding from Session 9's security review

13 more Netlify Function HTTP endpoints remain to expose the rest of the persistence layer (activity events, entitlements, subscriptions, technology assets, backups, reminders, IT support classification, checklists, metrics, templates, webhook events); retention/deletion, privacy content, remote-support enforcement, F060 AI, and F001-F060 workbook regeneration remain separately-scoped bodies of work
releaseRecommendation: NOT READY. All four resolvable owner decisions so far are done, and the full customer-facing commercial flow (ticket -> scope -> change order -> approval -> payment) now works end to end over real HTTP endpoints against the live database. 42 functions have real tested logic, 30 with real Postgres persistence, 541 passing unit tests, 9 HTTP endpoints live-verified. Remaining work is substantial and itemized, not estimated as a single number: 13 more endpoints, MFA for admin, data retention/deletion/legal-hold system, Privacy Policy content rewrite, remote-support/automation boundary enforcement, F060 AI implementation, F001-F060 workbook regeneration, and a UI.
